rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical mediator-induced intermolecular selective (3+2) annulation of allylmalonates with alkynes/alkenes to give cyclopentene/cyclopentane derivatives via radical intermediates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of mediator (I- to I• or Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+); the oxidized mediator then performs SET on deprotonated allylmalonate to generate a C-centered radical, while H2/methoxide or water is generated at the cathode

key_intermediates.name: malonyl carbon radical A from allylmalonate after deprotonation/SET
alkenyl/vinyl radical B from addition of A to phenylacetylene
cyclopentenyl carbon radical C (5-exo cyclization product)
cyclohexenyl radical D (6-endo cyclization product, thermodynamic)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I• (or Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+); the oxidized mediator removes an electron from the base-deprotonated allylmalonate to form malonyl radical A, which adds to the alkyne to give vinyl radical B, undergoes kinetically favored 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the pendant alkene to give cyclopentenyl radical C, which is trapped by I• (path a, NaI system) or abstracts H from solvent/1,4-CHD (path b, Cp2Fe system); the cathode reduces protons/methanol to H2 and methoxide.
rate_determining_step: Addition of malonyl radical A to phenylacetylene to give vinyl radical B (DFT barrier 14.6 kcal/mol); regioselectivity is set by the kinetically preferred 5-exo-trig cyclization TS-2 vs higher-barrier 6-endo TS-3

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction almost completely inhibited in both NaI and Cp2Fe systems; trace TEMPO adduct detected by GC-MS

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; no adduct detected

probe_reagents.name: N-iodosuccinimide (NIS)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 7 formed with or without electricity, ruling out I+ as the active species

probe_reagents.name: I2
probe_reagents.observation: No product 7 formed with or without electricity, ruling out molecular I2

probe_reagents.name: dimethyl 2-allyl-2-iodomalonate (1a-I)
probe_reagents.observation: No annulation under standard conditions; only 14% yield when added to anode in divided cell, ruling out 1a-I as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 2-((phenylsulfonyl)methyl)acrylate
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped alkyl radical C addition product detected by HRMS in Cp2Fe system

probe_reagents.name: 2-methylthiophene
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped radical C arylation product detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: 1a-D (deuterated allylmalonate)
probe_reagents.observation: Only non-deuterated product 75 detected; 75-D not observed, indicating the exocyclic methyl H comes from solvent/1,4-CHD rather than from 1a

notes: Two mediator systems give complementary products: NaI affords iodo-cyclopentenes (path a, I• traps radical C); Cp2Fe with 1,4-CHD as H-source affords protodemetalated cyclopentenes (path b, HAT from 1,4-CHD/solvent). DFT shows the 6-endo TS-3 leads to thermodynamically more stable D but has a 5.8 kcal/mol higher barrier than 5-exo TS-2, so selectivity is kinetic. CV confirms catalytic current of mediator + 1a + base. EPR signal (g=2.0073, AαN=14.30 G, AβH=23.60 G) supports a trapped carbon radical.
